hard_constraints.base_reference: faithful12 base remains the base reference
hard_constraints.continuity_rule: exact same mechanism state before resume
hard_constraints.search_scope: very small anchor9 butterfly-only local micro-batch

reference_candidate_specs.rationale: Prompt reference point: original same-sign butterfly with uniform d6 dwell.
Prompt reference point: original flip-sign butterfly with uniform d6 dwell.
Current best-known mean-oriented butterfly incumbent before this micro-batch.
Current best-known balanced flip-sign butterfly incumbent before this micro-batch.

microbatch_specs.rationale: Reverse the diagonal visit order while keeping the same-sign sweep pattern and the same total dwell budget (8/8). If the loop is order-sensitive, this should move the branch even with identical total time.
Reverse the diagonal visit order for the flip-sign butterfly at the same 8/8 dwell level.
Bias dwell toward the entry-side diagonal (10/6 instead of 8/8) while preserving exact closure and total time. This checks whether the butterfly should load more strongly before the cross-over.

Bias dwell toward the resume-side diagonal (6/10 instead of 8/8). This is the most direct check of whether the second diagonal visit should carry more weight because it is closest to the anchor10 resume boundary.

Keep the same-sign d8 butterfly and total time fixed, but shift one second of hold from the opening edge to the final closing edge (open/cross/close = 2/3/4). This is a mild asymmetry tweak that minimally increases settling time immediately before node10 resumes while preserving exact continuity.

microbatch_markov42_rows.rationale: Bias dwell toward the entry-side diagonal (10/6 instead of 8/8) while preserving exact closure and total time. This checks whether the butterfly should load more strongly before the cross-over.

Keep the same-sign d8 butterfly and total time fixed, but shift one second of hold from the opening edge to the final closing edge (open/cross/close = 2/3/4). This is a mild asymmetry tweak that minimally increases settling time immediately before node10 resumes while preserving exact continuity.

Reverse the diagonal visit order while keeping the same-sign sweep pattern and the same total dwell budget (8/8). If the loop is order-sensitive, this should move the branch even with identical total time.
Reverse the diagonal visit order for the flip-sign butterfly at the same 8/8 dwell level.

Bias dwell toward the resume-side diagonal (6/10 instead of 8/8). This is the most direct check of whether the second diagonal visit should carry more weight because it is closest to the anchor10 resume boundary.

best_butterfly_candidate.rationale: Bias dwell toward the entry-side diagonal (10/6 instead of 8/8) while preserving exact closure and total time. This checks whether the butterfly should load more strongly before the cross-over.

hypothesis_findings.finding: Reversing the butterfly order did improve the family incumbent. Best order-reversal candidate `probe5_l9_diag_bfly_same_rev_d8` landed at 11.275 / 0.987 / 99.944, which is Δmean +0.005 vs same_d8.

The better split candidate was `probe5_l9_diag_bfly_same_split_10_6` at 11.109 / 1.147 / 99.938. This is Δmean +0.171 and Δmax +0.003 vs same_d8, so the 8/8 incumbent was displaced by a split variant.
The close-biased asymmetry candidate landed at 11.237 / 1.071 / 99.942, with Δmean +0.043 and Δmax -0.001 vs same_d8.

The only boundary-touching test was the close-bias timing shift. It changed only the hold allocation immediately before node10 resumed, kept the mechanism state exactly identical, and therefore cleanly isolated resume-boundary sensitivity without introducing any new illegal motion.

verdict: best butterfly remains `probe5_l9_diag_bfly_same_split_10_6` at 11.109 / 1.147 / 99.938; the micro-batch did displace the current family incumbent.
competition_verdict: borderline: improved locally but still not a full competing family
competition_explanation: The micro-batch displaced the previous same_d8 incumbent with `probe5_l9_diag_bfly_same_split_10_6` at 11.109 / 1.147 / 99.938. Relative to old best legal this is Δmean -0.095, Δmax -0.016; relative to the unified winner it is Δmean -1.186, Δmax -0.305.
